fixture.active_mode: Test-only harness mode selector: battle | neutral. This is the only activation switch; default should remain battle so the maintained battle path stays unchanged.

neutral.objective_point_xy: Single objective point as [x, y] for fixture-only objective injection. This is not canonical runtime target semantics.
neutral.stop_radius: Neutral-only objective termination radius around objective_point_xy. It is used for objective-reached judgment and late clamp/termination semantics; it is not the battle hold mechanism.
neutral.fleet_archetype_id: Single-fleet archetype top-level key in archetypes/archetypes_v1_5.json (or 'default').
neutral.fleet_size: Single-fleet initial unit count for neutral.
neutral.aspect_ratio: Single-fleet initial formation col/row ratio for neutral.
neutral.origin_xy: Single-fleet initial formation center anchor as [x, y] for neutral.
neutral.facing_angle_deg: Single-fleet initial facing angle in degrees for neutral. 0=+X, 90=+Y.

fleet.fleet_a_archetype_id: Fleet A archetype top-level key in archetypes/archetypes_v1_5.json (or 'default').
fleet.fleet_b_archetype_id: Fleet B archetype top-level key in archetypes/archetypes_v1_5.json (or 'default').
fleet.initial_fleet_a_size: Initial unit count for fleet A.
fleet.initial_fleet_b_size: Initial unit count for fleet B.
fleet.initial_fleet_a_aspect_ratio: Fleet A formation col/row ratio. Example: 2.0 means wider than tall. Each row is centered by its actual row size, including the final partial row.
fleet.initial_fleet_b_aspect_ratio: Fleet B formation col/row ratio. Example: 2.0 means wider than tall. Each row is centered by its actual row size, including the final partial row.
fleet.initial_fleet_a_origin_xy: Preferred Fleet A initial formation center anchor as [x, y]. If present, overrides legacy initial_fleet_a_origin_x / initial_fleet_a_origin_y.
fleet.initial_fleet_b_origin_xy: Preferred Fleet B initial formation center anchor as [x, y]. If present, overrides legacy initial_fleet_b_origin_x / initial_fleet_b_origin_y.
fleet.initial_fleet_a_origin_x: Fleet A initial formation center anchor X coordinate.
fleet.initial_fleet_a_origin_y: Fleet A initial formation center anchor Y coordinate.
fleet.initial_fleet_b_origin_x: Fleet B initial formation center anchor X coordinate.
fleet.initial_fleet_b_origin_y: Fleet B initial formation center anchor Y coordinate.
fleet.initial_fleet_a_facing_angle_deg: Fleet A initial facing angle in degrees. 0=+X, 90=+Y.
fleet.initial_fleet_b_facing_angle_deg: Fleet B initial facing angle in degrees. 0=+X, 90=+Y.

visualization.display_language: Display language for human-facing fleet/commander names used by the maintained 3D replay path. Supported values: EN | ZH.
visualization.print_tick_summary: Print per-tick summary logs to console.

metatype.settings_path: Path to metatype config (default: archetypes/metatype_settings.json; Yang display/identity template plus scenario-level sampling rules). These sampled personality values are retained only on the high-level prepared surface and do not enter maintained runtime mechanisms.
metatype.random_seed: Random seed for scenario-level metatype/Yang sampling. If omitted, falls back to run_control.random_seed. If -1, use true randomness. This seed does not activate any runtime personality mechanism.

selectors.movement_model: Runtime movement selector for the maintained test_run mainline. The public selector is now v4a only; baseline and v3a have been retired.

restore.strength: Active v4a restore-strength seam in [0.0, 1.0]. Current v4a read is direct: restore_term = restore_strength * normalize(restore_vector). This seam no longer uses formation_rigidity, pursuit_drive, mobility_bias, or any hidden native scale. Current v4a movement also no longer depends on the old runtime cohesion decision source (`v2` vs `v3_test`) for this seam.

reference.expected_reference_spacing: Expected/reference formation spacing for the v4a line. This is distinct from runtime.physical.movement_low_level.min_unit_spacing, which remains the physical-layer minimum spacing.
reference.layout_mode: Reference formation layout mode for the v4a line. Supported values are rect_centered_1.0 and rect_centered_4.0. This target reference aspect is distinct from the fleet's initial spawned aspect ratio.
reference.surface_mode: Current v4a-internal reference-surface seam. It remains candidate-specific because future movement candidates may coexist with v4a for a time; it is not a general movement/formation owner.
reference.soft_morphology_relaxation: Fleet-level morphology relaxation for the soft_morphology_v1 carrier in (0.0, 1.0]. Higher values contract toward the alive-ratio target envelope faster; lower values keep the morphology response softer and more continuous.

transition.shape_vs_advance_strength: Continuous v4a transition seam in [0.0, 1.0]. Higher values reduce pure objective-chasing more aggressively when morphology error is large, leaving more movement budget for shape transition before arrival/hold.
transition.heading_relaxation: Continuous v4a heading-realization seam in (0.0, 1.0]. Higher values let the fleet heading follow desired target direction faster; lower values keep heading change more inertial and reduce abrupt transition pivots.

battle.standoff_hold_band_ratio: Fleet-level battle standoff hold-band ratio in [0.0, 1.0]. Sets the width of the near-d* no-chase band as a fraction of attack_range.
battle.target_front_strip_gap_bias: Single v4a near-contact correction bias added to the base target front-strip gap. Current base read is max(0, fire_optimal_range - expected_reference_spacing), where fire_optimal_range = attack_range * fire_optimal_range_ratio. Negative values bring the battle relation inward; positive values push it outward. This is the last-stage correction term on target gap semantics, not a scaling of fleet-body geometry or of the final control error.
battle.hold_weight_strength: Battle-first reversible hold suppression strength in [0.0, 1.0]. 0.0 disables d*-hold suppression entirely; 1.0 applies the full bounded near-d* approach suppression. If fleets are pulled far apart again, hold weakens automatically and pre-contact-like approach resumes.
battle.relation_lead_ticks: Near-contact signed-relation lead window in ticks. Together with unit max_speed, this sets how early fleets begin to decelerate before target front-strip gap is reached.
battle.hold_relaxation: Raw-to-current relaxation for the near-contact v4a relation family in (0.0, 1.0]. This smooths battle_relation_gap, close_drive, brake_drive, and hold_weight before they affect active battle behavior.
battle.approach_drive_relaxation: Raw-to-current relaxation for the near-contact v4a approach-drive path in (0.0, 1.0]. Kept separate so forward approach authority can lag or follow the signed relation at a different pace than hold/brake state.
battle.near_contact_internal_stability_blend: Near-contact internal-speed unification blend in [0.0, 1.0]. Scales how strongly hold-weight pulls forward_transport_speed_scale back toward 1.0 and shape_speed_scale back toward a fleet-level advance floor while battle relation is active.
battle.near_contact_speed_relaxation: Per-unit near-contact speed relaxation in (0.0, 1.0]. Smooths unit.max_speed toward transition_speed_target after the internal-stability blend has adjusted per-unit speed factors.

engagement.engaged_speed_scale: Current v4a candidate seam for engaged-movement speed envelope in (0.0, 1.0]. Even forward-attacking engaged units move at this fraction or below of their transition-allowed speed.
engagement.attack_speed_lateral_scale: Current v4a candidate seam for attack-direction movement allowance when engaged attack direction is lateral to current orientation. Forward attack interpolates from this value up toward 1.0.
engagement.attack_speed_backward_scale: Current v4a candidate seam for attack-direction movement allowance when engaged attack direction is backward relative to current orientation. Must be <= attack_speed_lateral_scale.

boundary.enabled: Enable boundary soft mechanism master switch. false=soft/hard both OFF. true=soft ON and hard follows hard_enabled.
boundary.soft_strength: Boundary soft-force strength multiplier. 0.0 disables soft push while enabled=true; 1.0 keeps current baseline.
boundary.hard_enabled: Enable hard boundary clamp only when enabled=true. true=hard clamp ON; false=soft-only boundary mode.

movement_low_level.min_unit_spacing: Physical-layer minimum spacing used by local separation / projection / related low-level runtime behavior. This is distinct from any v4a test-only expected/reference formation spacing candidate.
movement_low_level.alpha_sep: Soft-separation / boundary contribution weight inside movement composition. Current default=0.6. Runtime clamps to >=0.0.

fire_control.fire_quality_alpha: Directional fire-quality coefficient alpha. 0.0 disables directional effect. Runtime currently reads angle quality as max(0, 1 + alpha * cos(theta)), so front-facing quality is 1+alpha and fully backward quality is 1-alpha before other modifiers.
fire_control.fire_optimal_range_ratio: Range-quality optimal-distance ratio in [0.0, 1.0]. attack_range remains max range; this ratio defines the distance below which range_quality stays at 1.0 and beyond which it decays linearly to 0.0 at attack_range.
fire_control.alpha_safe_max: Documentation-level current runtime clamp upper bound for fire_quality_alpha. Runtime now clamps alpha within [0.0, 1.0].

contact.contact_hysteresis_h: Contact hysteresis band ratio h. Runtime clamp is [0.0, 0.2]. h<=0 disables CH.

hostile_contact_impedance.active_mode: Test-only hostile contact-impedance selector: off | hybrid_v2.

hybrid_v2.radius_multiplier: v2 hybrid local influence radius as a multiplier of min_unit_spacing.
hybrid_v2.repulsion_max_disp_ratio: v2 hybrid-only per-tick max hostile-repulsion displacement as a ratio of min_unit_spacing.
hybrid_v2.forward_damping_strength: v2 hybrid damping fraction removable from positive forward displacement at full local hostile proximity.

observer.tick_timing_enabled: Observer telemetry toggle for per-tick wall-clock elapsed time recording inside test_run. true records tick_elapsed_ms for each simulation tick; false skips timing capture. This is the single maintained runtime.observer switch and does not change battle semantics.

run_control.max_time_steps: Maximum simulation ticks. If <=0, run until the first resolved stop condition (battle elimination or fixture objective arrival), then keep running for run_control.post_resolution_hold_steps more ticks, capped at 999 ticks.
run_control.post_resolution_hold_steps: Extra ticks to keep running after the first resolved stop condition. Used for both battle winner hold and neutral objective-arrival hold. Must be >= 0.
run_control.random_seed: General visualization seed (non-map). If -1, use true randomness.
run_control.symmetric_movement_sync_enabled: Harness-side symmetric movement merge switch. This is a run-level execution control that removes cross-fleet sequential-update bias without touching frozen runtime.
run_control.observer_enabled: Run-level observer/diagnostic capture switch for maintained test_run surfaces. true enables maintained observer telemetry and diagnostic exports; false keeps the run headless/quiet unless another explicit export path is requested.

battlefield.background_map_seed: Background map seed (stars/planet/asteroid field). If -1, use true randomness.

unit.attack_range: Combat hit range (distance threshold). Phase V governance default is 5.0.
unit.damage_per_tick: Base attack damage per successful hit event.
unit.unit_max_hit_points: Initial and max HP per unit.
